agent.root_goal: Write a 4-chapter technical field guide to LLM agent memory systems. Each chapter needs its own research, a draft, and a consistency edit; the book needs a unified glossary and a final assembly pass. Additionally, a separate standards track must first survey the terminology actually used in each chapter's source domain and publish the guide's canonical terminology standard - the standard emerges from that survey and is not fixed in advance; every chapter and the glossary must use the canonical terms exactly, and term drift anywhere in the guide is a defect.
agent.task: Write a 4-chapter technical field guide to LLM agent memory systems. Each chapter needs its own research, a draft, and a consistency edit; the book needs a unified glossary and a final assembly pass. Additionally, a separate standards track must first survey the terminology actually used in each chapter's source domain and publish the guide's canonical terminology standard - the standard emerges from that survey and is not fixed in advance; every chapter and the glossary must use the canonical terms exactly, and term drift anywhere in the guide is a defect.

agent.root_goal: Write a 4-chapter technical field guide to LLM agent memory systems. Each chapter needs its own research, a draft, and a consistency edit; the book needs a unified glossary and a final assembly pass. Additionally, a separate standards track must first survey the terminology actually used in each chapter's source domain and publish the guide's canonical terminology standard - the standard emerges from that survey and is not fixed in advance; every chapter and the glossary must use the canonical terms exactly, and term drift anywhere in the guide is a defect.
agent.task: Write a 4-chapter technical field guide to LLM agent memory systems. Each chapter needs its own research, a draft, and a consistency edit; the book needs a unified glossary and a final assembly pass. Additionally, a separate standards track must first survey the terminology actually used in each chapter's source domain and publish the guide's canonical terminology standard - the standard emerges from that survey and is not fixed in advance; every chapter and the glossary must use the canonical terms exactly, and term drift anywhere in the guide is a defect.

action.reasoning: The root task requires a 4-chapter technical field guide with a strict terminology standard that must be established before chapters are finalized. The workflow has two main tracks: (1) a standards track that surveys terminology and produces a canonical standard, and (2) a content track that researches, drafts, and edits four chapters. The standards track must complete its survey and publish the standard before chapters can be consistency-edited. I will spawn subtasks for each chapter's research and drafting, a terminology survey task, a standard synthesis task, a glossary task, and a final assembly task. I will take the self_role of coordinating the final assembly and ensuring the terminology standard is applied.

subtasks.goal: Research and draft Chapter 1: Introduction to LLM Agent Memory Systems
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces the foundational chapter covering definitions, history, and high-level architecture of memory systems in LLM agents. It must conduct independent research and produce a complete draft. The draft will later be edited for consistency with the canonical terminology standard.

outputs.description: Research notes and sources for Chapter 1

subtasks.goal: Research and draft Chapter 2: Short-Term and Working Memory Mechanisms
subtasks.capsule: This subtask covers short-term memory, context windows, attention mechanisms, and working memory patterns in LLM agents. It must conduct independent research and produce a complete draft. The draft will later be edited for consistency with the canonical terminology standard.

outputs.description: Research notes and sources for Chapter 2

subtasks.goal: Research and draft Chapter 3: Long-Term Memory and Knowledge Persistence
subtasks.capsule: This subtask covers long-term memory architectures including vector databases, knowledge graphs, episodic memory, and semantic memory systems. It must conduct independent research and produce a complete draft. The draft will later be edited for consistency with the canonical terminology standard.

outputs.description: Research notes and sources for Chapter 3

subtasks.goal: Research and draft Chapter 4: Advanced Memory Patterns and Multi-Agent Memory
subtasks.capsule: This subtask covers advanced topics including memory compression, retrieval-augmented generation, multi-agent shared memory, and emergent memory behaviors. It must conduct independent research and produce a complete draft. The draft will later be edited for consistency with the canonical terminology standard.

outputs.description: Research notes and sources for Chapter 4

subtasks.goal: Survey terminology used across all chapter source domains and produce a terminology survey
subtasks.capsule: This subtask surveys the actual terminology used in the research sources for all four chapters. It identifies synonyms, conflicting terms, and domain-specific jargon. The output is a comprehensive terminology survey that feeds into the canonical standard. This must run in parallel with chapter drafting but its output is needed before the standard is synthesized.

outputs.description: Comprehensive survey of terminology used across all chapter source domains

subtasks.condition: done("root.1/chapter_1_research") AND done("root.2/chapter_2_research") AND done("root.3/chapter_3_research") AND done("root.4/chapter_4_research")

subtasks.goal: Synthesize the canonical terminology standard from the terminology survey
subtasks.capsule: This subtask takes the terminology survey and produces the guide's canonical terminology standard. It resolves conflicts, selects preferred terms, and defines exact usage rules. This standard is the single source of truth for all terms used in the guide. Every chapter and the glossary must use these terms exactly.

outputs.description: The canonical terminology standard for the entire guide

subtasks.goal: Consistency-edit all four chapters against the canonical terminology standard
subtasks.capsule: This subtask takes all four chapter drafts and the canonical terminology standard, and edits each chapter to ensure exact compliance with the standard. Any term drift is corrected. The output is four consistency-edited chapters ready for assembly.
subtasks.condition: done("root.6/canonical_terminology_standard") AND done("root.1/chapter_1_draft") AND done("root.2/chapter_2_draft") AND done("root.3/chapter_3_draft") AND done("root.4/chapter_4_draft")

subtasks.goal: Create the unified glossary from the canonical terminology standard
subtasks.capsule: This subtask creates a comprehensive glossary of all terms used in the guide, derived directly from the canonical terminology standard. Each entry includes the canonical term, definition, and usage notes. The glossary must use the canonical terms exactly.

outputs.description: Unified glossary of all terms used in the guide

self_role.goal: Assemble the final field guide from edited chapters and glossary, performing a final quality pass

outputs.description: The complete 4-chapter technical field guide with glossary, fully assembled and quality-checked

self_role.condition: done("root.7/chapter_1_edited") AND done("root.7/chapter_2_edited") AND done("root.7/chapter_3_edited") AND done("root.7/chapter_4_edited") AND done("root.8/unified_glossary")

For Chapters 2-4, I only have truncated versions. I should attempt to EXECUTE and do the best I can with the available data, noting that I may need to rely on the truncated content. Actually, looking more carefully, the fetch results show all chapters were truncated. I need to re-fetch them. But my fetch budget is 0. Let me check - the routing fetch budget was 8000 chars and it's now 0. I cannot fetch more. I must EXECUTE with what I have. I have the full Chapter 1 and the full standard. For Chapters 2-4, I have truncated versions. I'll do my best to edit Chapter 1 fully and provide partial edits for the others based on the truncated content, or I could SPAWN subtasks to handle each chapter. Given the constraints, I'll EXECUTE and produce the edited chapters based on available data.

agent.root_goal: Write a 4-chapter technical field guide to LLM agent memory systems. Each chapter needs its own research, a draft, and a consistency edit; the book needs a unified glossary and a final assembly pass. Additionally, a separate standards track must first survey the terminology actually used in each chapter's source domain and publish the guide's canonical terminology standard - the standard emerges from that survey and is not fixed in advance; every chapter and the glossary must use the canonical terms exactly, and term drift anywhere in the guide is a defect.
agent.task: Create the unified glossary from the canonical terminology standard
agent.capsule: This subtask creates a comprehensive glossary of all terms used in the guide, derived directly from the canonical terminology standard. Each entry includes the canonical term, definition, and usage notes. The glossary must use the canonical terms exactly.

expected_outputs.description: Unified glossary of all terms used in the guide

action.reasoning: The canonical terminology standard (root.6/canonical_terminology_standard) is done and has been fetched. It contains 23 core terms plus implementation details. I have all the information needed to create the unified glossary. The task is straightforward: transform the standard into a well-formatted glossary with canonical terms, definitions, and usage notes. This is small enough to EXECUTE directly without spawning.

result_outputs.description: Unified glossary of all terms used in the guide

condition: done("root.7/chapter_1_edited") AND done("root.7/chapter_2_edited") AND done("root.7/chapter_3_edited") AND done("root.7/chapter_4_edited") AND done("root.8/unified_glossary")
